steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank connects two strategies for naming a band. The first strategy values being “unique and original.” The second strategy involves naming the band after a recognizable song — the opposite approach. The transition must signal a contrast between two equally valid alternatives.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "Alternatively,": Introduces a different option of equal standing, matching the shift from one valid strategy to an opposing one. ✓
  • "Thus,": Signals a conclusion or result. The second strategy is not a result of the first. ✗
  • "In other words,": Signals restatement. The second strategy is not a restatement of the first — it contradicts it. ✗
  • "For instance,": Signals an example of what came before. Naming after a recognizable song would illustrate being “unique and original,” which is wrong. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
